title_en: Enduring Authority and Value for Muwatta Studies
content_en:
At-Tamhid has been recognized since its composition as the definitive scholarly commentary on the Muwatta of Imam Malik, and its authority has not been seriously challenged across the nine centuries since Ibn Abd al-Barr completed it. No subsequent commentary on the Muwatta approaches it in comprehensiveness, and scholars who write on Muwatta-related topics invariably cite At-Tamhid as the primary reference for both the chains and the meanings of the traditions it addresses.

The biographical scholarship in At-Tamhid has had an influence extending well beyond Muwatta studies. Ibn Abd al-Barr's biographical notices for the narrators of the Muwatta — which collectively cover most of the major figures of the first three generations of Islam — have been cited extensively by subsequent biographical scholars including al-Mizzi in Tahdhib al-Kamal and Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani in Tahdhib at-Tahdhib. His assessments of individual narrators carry significant authority in the field of rijal criticism.

The cross-school legal analysis in At-Tamhid has made it a reference for scholars of all four legal schools, not only Malikis. Hanafi, Shafi'i, and Hanbali scholars have regularly cited Ibn Abd al-Barr's discussions of legal disputes as authoritative presentations of the range of early scholarly opinion, even when they ultimately disagree with his conclusions. His reputation for fairness in presenting other schools' positions has given his work a credibility across the schools that few explicitly school-committed commentaries achieve.

For contemporary scholars of Islamic jurisprudence and hadith, At-Tamhid remains an essential resource. Its combination of isnad analysis, biographical scholarship, linguistic commentary, and cross-school legal comparison in a single work makes it unique in the tradition. Reading it requires investment — its organization by narrator rather than by topic makes thematic navigation challenging without an index — but the rewards are proportionate to the effort. Modern indexed editions have made systematic study of At-Tamhid more accessible than at any previous point in the work's history.
title_id: Otoritas Abadi dan Nilai untuk Studi Al-Muwatta
content_id:
At-Tamhid telah diakui sejak disusunnya sebagai komentar ilmiah definitif atas Al-Muwatta karya Imam Malik, dan otoritasnya tidak pernah dipertanyakan secara serius selama sembilan abad sejak Ibn Abd al-Barr menyelesaikannya. Tidak ada komentar berikutnya tentang Al-Muwatta yang mendekatinya dalam hal komprehensivitas, dan para ulama yang menulis tentang topik-topik terkait Muwatta secara tak terelakkan mengutip At-Tamhid sebagai referensi utama untuk sanad maupun makna tradisi-tradisi yang ditanganinya.

Keilmuan biografi dalam At-Tamhid memiliki pengaruh yang melampaui studi Muwatta. Catatan-catatan biografi Ibn Abd al-Barr untuk para perawi Al-Muwatta — yang secara kolektif mencakup sebagian besar tokoh-tokoh utama tiga generasi pertama Islam — telah dikutip secara luas oleh para ulama biografi berikutnya termasuk al-Mizzi dalam Tahdzib al-Kamal dan Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani dalam Tahdzib at-Tahdzib. Penilaian-penilaiannya tentang masing-masing perawi membawa otoritas yang signifikan dalam bidang kritik rijal.

Analisis hukum lintas madzhab dalam At-Tamhid telah menjadikannya referensi bagi ulama-ulama dari semua empat madzhab hukum, bukan hanya Maliki. Para ulama Hanafi, Syafi'i, dan Hanbali secara rutin mengutip diskusi-diskusi Ibn Abd al-Barr tentang perselisihan hukum sebagai penyajian yang otoritatif tentang rentang pendapat ilmiah awal, bahkan ketika mereka pada akhirnya tidak setuju dengan kesimpulan-kesimpulannya. Reputasinya karena kejujuran dalam menyajikan posisi-posisi madzhab lain telah memberikan karyanya kredibilitas lintas madzhab yang langka dalam literatur hukum Islam klasik.
